result.title: Compile-time queryable chain rewriter (provider-pluggable Roslyn feature)

answers.answer: (a) Attribute on the source/provider type (e.g., [CompileTimeQueryable("…Rewriter")] on Postgres9Table) — mirrors [Quotable]
(b) Follow simple local-variable dataflow within a method until terminator

Materializers: ToArray/ToList/ToDictionary/ToHashSet
Aggregates / single-result: Single/First/Last/Count/Any/All/Sum/Min/Max/Average (+ *OrDefault)
foreach over the chain
await on the chain (ToArrayAsync, etc.)
(a) Operator-agnostic — chain = any call returning the same marked queryable type; rewriter decides what it translates

answers.customAnswer: I think about the tree of possible chains with branches
answers.answer: I think about the tree of possible chains with branches
(a) C# source text — Quotable-style substitution (inline when valid, wrap in synthesized static method otherwise)
(b) Silent fallback — chain runs at runtime through the type's normal IQueryable/IEnumerable plumbing if any
(a) Analyzer mechanism + assembly-qualified name in the attribute (mirrors Quotable)

Canonical sample rewriter — Postgres-style table → SQL + Dapper-style materializer (end-to-end)
Compiler tests — binding, emit (inlined + wrapped), error scenarios (missing rewriter, decline, invalid output)
Language design spec under docs/specs/
Benchmarks — compile-time overhead per chain + runtime overhead per call site vs runtime IQueryable

VB.NET support
Shared plumbing with the [Quotable] feature
IDE refactorings / IntelliSense extensions / 'view emitted code' UI
Reimplementing System.Linq.Expressions.Expression<T> via this mechanism
